rationale: The citing paper’s core question is about promotion spillovers, crowding out, and total-sales opportunity costs, while the cited paper is also a randomized online field-experiment study centered on advertising spillovers and implications for advertiser returns. Given this close construct and design alignment (both causal field experiments on spillovers in digital marketplaces), the citation is plausibly substantive rather than incidental. The pair therefore warrants full relation judgment despite only one DOI-level matched reference record.
